description: Create Analytics endpoints and upgrade Dashboard to include data from new tables (visitors, maintenance). Add real database aggregation queries for revenue trends, occupancy, complaint analytics, and attendance trends.
steps: Create AnalyticsController at backend/src/main/java/com/hostel/controller/AnalyticsController.java with endpoints: GET /api/analytics/summary, GET /api/analytics/revenue, GET /api/analytics/occupancy, GET /api/analytics/complaints, GET /api/analytics/attendance. All return ResponseEntity<ApiResponse<T>>.

Create AnalyticsService at backend/src/main/java/com/hostel/service/AnalyticsService.java that queries all repositories for aggregated data. Summary: total counts from all tables. Revenue: monthly fee collection using @Query with GROUP BY month. Occupancy: room status counts. Complaints: counts by status and category. Attendance: percentage by date range.

Create DTO classes for analytics responses in backend/src/main/java/com/hostel/dto/: AnalyticsSummaryDTO.java (all high-level counts), RevenueAnalyticsDTO.java (monthly amounts list), OccupancyAnalyticsDTO.java (occupied/available/maintenance counts), ComplaintAnalyticsDTO.java (by status and category breakdowns), AttendanceAnalyticsDTO.java (daily/weekly percentages).

Add new @Query methods to existing repositories: FeeRepository - monthly revenue grouped by year-month; AttendanceRepository - count by status and date range; ComplaintRepository - count grouped by category. Add count methods to the new repositories: MaintenanceRequestRepository countByStatus, VisitorRepository countByStatus.
Update DashboardStatsDTO.java to add new fields: activeVisitors (long), todayAttendancePercentage (double), openMaintenanceRequests (long), upcomingEvents (long), activeNotices (long).

Update DashboardService.java to query the new repositories (VisitorRepository, MaintenanceRequestRepository, EventRepository, NoticeRepository, AttendanceRepository) and populate the new DashboardStatsDTO fields with real database counts.
acceptance_criteria: All analytics data comes from real JPA repository queries with @Query annotations where needed
DashboardStatsDTO includes activeVisitors, todayAttendancePercentage, openMaintenanceRequests, upcomingEvents, activeNotices
DashboardService injects and queries all new repositories
Backend compiles successfully with mvn compile -DskipTests
findings: All analytics code compiles cleanly with Java 17. Used JPQL with YEAR()/MONTH()/LPAD()/CAST() functions for H2-compatible monthly revenue grouping. AttendanceRepository uses date range grouped query returning Object[] for flexible aggregation in service layer. DashboardService now injects all 9 repositories.
